question_text: Find the area of the region bounded by the curves y 2 = 9x, y = 3x.
question_html: Find the area of the region bounded by the curves y2 = 9x, y = 3x.
solution_text: We have y 2 = 9x and y = 3x {tex}\Rightarrow {\left( {3x} \right)^2} = 9x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 9{x^2} - 9x = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 9x\left( {x - 1} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x = 1,0{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area, {tex}A = \int\limits_0^1 {\sqrt {9x} dx - \int\limits_0^1 {3xdx} } {/tex} {tex} = 3\int_0^1 {{x^{1/2}}dx - 3\int_0^1 {xdx} } {/tex} {tex}= 3\left[ {\frac{{{x^{3/2}}}}{{3/2}}} \right]_0^1 - 3\left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_0^1{/tex} {tex}= 3\left( {\frac{2}{3} - 0} \right) - 3\left( {\frac{1}{2} - 0} \right){/tex} {tex} = 2 - \frac{3}{2} = \frac{1}{2}{/tex} sq units
solution_html:
We have y2 = 9x and y = 3x
{tex}\Rightarrow {\left( {3x} \right)^2} = 9x{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow 9{x^2} - 9x = 0{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow 9x\left( {x - 1} \right) = 0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow x = 1,0{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area, {tex}A = \int\limits_0^1 {\sqrt {9x} dx - \int\limits_0^1 {3xdx} } {/tex}
{tex} = 3\int_0^1 {{x^{1/2}}dx - 3\int_0^1 {xdx} } {/tex} 
{tex}= 3\left[ {\frac{{{x^{3/2}}}}{{3/2}}} \right]_0^1 - 3\left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_0^1{/tex} 
{tex}= 3\left( {\frac{2}{3} - 0} \right) - 3\left( {\frac{1}{2} - 0} \right){/tex} 
{tex} = 2 - \frac{3}{2} = \frac{1}{2}{/tex}sq units

question_text: Find the area of the region bounded by the parabola y 2 = 2px, x 2 = 2py
question_html: Find the area of the region bounded by the parabola y2 = 2px, x2 = 2py
solution_text: Given that, y 2 = 2px and x 2 = 2py Solving curves, we get {tex}\therefore{/tex} x 4 = 4p 2 y 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 4 = 4p 2 (2px) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 4 = 8p 3 x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x(x 3 - 8p 3 ) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 0, 2p When x = 0, y = 0 and when x = 2p, y = 2p So, points of intersection are (0, 0) and (2p, 2p) Graph of both the parabolas is as shown in the following figure. From the figure, Area of shaded region, A = {tex}\int_{0}^{2 p}\left(\sqrt{2 p x}-\frac{x^{2}}{2 p}\right) d x{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{2 p} \int_{0}^{2 p} x^{1 / 2} d x-\frac{1}{2 p} \int_{0}^{2 p} x^{2} d x{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{2 p}\left[\frac{2}{3} x^{3 / 2}\right]_{0}^{2 p}-\frac{1}{2 p}\left[\frac{x^{3}}{2}\right]_{0}^{2 p}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{2 p}\left(\frac{2}{3} \cdot 2 \sqrt{2} p^{3 / 2}\right)-\frac{1}{2 p}\left(\frac{1}{3} 8 p^{3}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{8}{3} p^{2}-\frac{4}{3} p^{2}=\frac{4 p^{2}}{3}{/tex} sq. units
solution_html:
Given that,
y2 = 2px and x2 = 2py
Solving curves, we get
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x4 = 4p2y2 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x4 = 4p2 (2px)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x4 = 8p3 x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x(x3 - 8p3) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 0, 2p
When x = 0, y = 0 and when x = 2p, y = 2p
So, points of intersection are (0, 0) and (2p, 2p)
Graph of both the parabolas is as shown in the following figure.

From the figure, Area of shaded region,
A = {tex}\int_{0}^{2 p}\left(\sqrt{2 p x}-\frac{x^{2}}{2 p}\right) d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt{2 p} \int_{0}^{2 p} x^{1 / 2} d x-\frac{1}{2 p} \int_{0}^{2 p} x^{2} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt{2 p}\left[\frac{2}{3} x^{3 / 2}\right]_{0}^{2 p}-\frac{1}{2 p}\left[\frac{x^{3}}{2}\right]_{0}^{2 p}{/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt{2 p}\left(\frac{2}{3} \cdot 2 \sqrt{2} p^{3 / 2}\right)-\frac{1}{2 p}\left(\frac{1}{3} 8 p^{3}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{8}{3} p^{2}-\frac{4}{3} p^{2}=\frac{4 p^{2}}{3}{/tex} sq. units

question_text: Find the area of the region bounded by the curve y = x 3 and y = x + 6 and x = 0.
question_html: Find the area of the region bounded by the curve y = x3 and y = x + 6 and x = 0.
solution_text: We have, y = x 3 and y = x + 6 and x = 0 {tex}\therefore {x^3} = x + 6{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {x^3} - x = 6{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {x^3} - x - 6 = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {x^2}\left( {x - 2} \right) + 2x\left( {x - 2} \right) + 3\left( {x - 2} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( {x - 2} \right)\left( {{x^2} + 2x + 3} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x = 2{/tex} with two imaginary points {tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area of shaded region {tex}= \int_0^2 {\left( {x + 6 - {x^3}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 6x - \frac{{{x^4}}}{4}} \right]_0^2{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{4}{2} + 12 - \frac{{16}}{4} - 0} \right]{/tex} = [2 + 12 - 4 ] =10 sq.units
solution_html:
We have, y = x3 and y = x + 6 and x = 0

{tex}\therefore {x^3} = x + 6{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow {x^3} - x = 6{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow {x^3} - x - 6 = 0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow {x^2}\left( {x - 2} \right) + 2x\left( {x - 2} \right) + 3\left( {x - 2} \right) = 0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \left( {x - 2} \right)\left( {{x^2} + 2x + 3} \right) = 0{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow x = 2{/tex} with two imaginary points
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area of shaded region {tex}= \int_0^2 {\left( {x + 6 - {x^3}} \right)dx} {/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 6x - \frac{{{x^4}}}{4}} \right]_0^2{/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {\frac{4}{2} + 12 - \frac{{16}}{4} - 0} \right]{/tex} 
= [2 + 12 - 4 ] =10 sq.units

question_text: Find the area of the region bounded by the curve y 2 = 4x, x 2 = 4y.
question_html: Find the area of the region bounded by the curve y2 = 4x, x2 = 4y.
solution_text: Given that y 2 = 4x and x 2 = 4y For intersection points, substitute y 2 = 4x or y = 2 {tex}\sqrt{x}{/tex} x 2 = 4y or x 2 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\sqrt{x}{/tex} or x 4 = 64x or x(x 3 - 64) = 0 or x = 0 and x = {tex}\pm{/tex} 4 When x = 0, y 2 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 0 or y = 0 When x = 4, y 2 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 or y = 4 {tex}\therefore{/tex} Points of intersection are (0, 0) and (4, 4). Given, y 2 = 4x or y = {tex}2 \sqrt{x}{/tex} = f(x) and y = {tex}\frac{1}{4} x^{2}{/tex} = g(x) where f(x) {tex}\ge{/tex} g(x) in (0, 4) {tex}\therefore{/tex} Required Area = {tex}\int_{0}^{4}[f(x)-g(x)] d x{/tex} = {tex}\left.\int_{0}^{4}\left[2 \sqrt{x}-\frac{1}{4} x^{2}\right)\right] d x{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac{4}{3} x^{3 / 2}-\frac{1}{12} x^{3}\right]_{0}^{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \times 4^{3 / 2}-\frac{1}{12} \times 4^{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{16}{3}{/tex} sq. units
solution_html:
Given that y2 = 4x and x2 = 4y

For intersection points, substitute
y2 = 4x or y = 2{tex}\sqrt{x}{/tex}
x2 = 4y
or x2 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\sqrt{x}{/tex} 
or x4 = 64x
or x(x3 - 64) = 0
or x = 0
and x = {tex}\pm{/tex} 4
When x = 0, y2 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 0 or y = 0
When x = 4, y2 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 or y = 4
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Points of intersection are (0, 0) and (4, 4).
Given, y2 = 4x or y = {tex}2 \sqrt{x}{/tex} = f(x)
and y = {tex}\frac{1}{4} x^{2}{/tex} = g(x)
where f(x) {tex}\ge{/tex} g(x) in (0, 4)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required Area = {tex}\int_{0}^{4}[f(x)-g(x)] d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\left.\int_{0}^{4}\left[2 \sqrt{x}-\frac{1}{4} x^{2}\right)\right] d x{/tex} 
= {tex}\left[\frac{4}{3} x^{3 / 2}-\frac{1}{12} x^{3}\right]_{0}^{4}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{4}{3} \times 4^{3 / 2}-\frac{1}{12} \times 4^{3}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{16}{3}{/tex} sq. units

question_text: Find the area of the region included between y 2 = 9x and y = x.
question_html: Find the area of the region included between y2 = 9x and y = x.
solution_text: We have, y 2 = 9x and y = x {tex} \Rightarrow {x^2} = 9x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} - 9x = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x\left( {x - 9} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x = 0,9{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of shaded region, {tex}A = \int_0^9 {\left( {\sqrt {9x} - x} \right)dx = \int_0^9 {3{x^{1/2}}dx - \int_0^9 {xdx} } } {/tex} {tex}= \left( {\frac{{6{x^{3/.2}}}}{3} - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right)_0^9{/tex} {tex}= \left( {6.\frac{{{9^{3/.2}}}}{3} - \frac{{81}}{2} - 0} \right){/tex} {tex}= 54 - \frac{{81}}{2} = \frac{{108 - 81}}{2} = \frac{{27}}{2}{/tex} sq units.
solution_html:
We have, y2 = 9x and y = x
{tex} \Rightarrow {x^2} = 9x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} - 9x = 0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow x\left( {x - 9} \right) = 0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow x = 0,9{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of shaded region, {tex}A = \int_0^9 {\left( {\sqrt {9x} - x} \right)dx = \int_0^9 {3{x^{1/2}}dx - \int_0^9 {xdx} } } {/tex} 
{tex}= \left( {\frac{{6{x^{3/.2}}}}{3} - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right)_0^9{/tex} 
{tex}= \left( {6.\frac{{{9^{3/.2}}}}{3} - \frac{{81}}{2} - 0} \right){/tex} 
{tex}= 54 - \frac{{81}}{2} = \frac{{108 - 81}}{2} = \frac{{27}}{2}{/tex} sq units.

question_text: Find the area of the region enclosed by the parabola x 2 = y and the line y = x + 2
question_html: Find the area of the region enclosed by the parabola x2 = y and the line y = x + 2
solution_text: We have, x 2 = y and y = x + 2 {tex} \Rightarrow {x^2} = x + 2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} - x - 2 = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} - 2x + x - 2 = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x\left( {x - 2} \right) + 1\left( {x - 2} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {x - 2} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x = - 1,2{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area of shaded region, {tex}= \int_{ - 1}^2 {\left( {x + 2 - {x^2}} \right)dx = \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 2x - \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}} \right]} _{ - 1}^2{/tex} {tex}= \left( {8 - 3 - \frac{1}{2}} \right) = \frac{9}{2}{/tex} sq. units
solution_html:
We have, x2 = y and y = x + 2
{tex} \Rightarrow {x^2} = x + 2{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} - x - 2 = 0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} - 2x + x - 2 = 0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow x\left( {x - 2} \right) + 1\left( {x - 2} \right) = 0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \left( {x + 1} \right)\left( {x - 2} \right) = 0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow x = - 1,2{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area of shaded region, {tex}= \int_{ - 1}^2 {\left( {x + 2 - {x^2}} \right)dx = \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 2x - \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}} \right]} _{ - 1}^2{/tex} 
{tex}= \left( {8 - 3 - \frac{1}{2}} \right) = \frac{9}{2}{/tex} sq. units

solution_text: We have, y 2 = 8x and x = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of shaded region, A = {tex} 2\int_\limits0^2 {\sqrt {8x} dx }{/tex} {tex}= 2.2\sqrt 2 \int_\limits0^2 {{x^{\frac{1}{2}}}dx}{/tex} {tex}= 4.\sqrt 2 .\left[ {\frac{{{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{\frac{3}{2}}} \right]_0^2 {/tex} {tex}= \frac{8}{3}\sqrt 2 \left[ {2^\frac{2}{3} - 0} \right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{8\sqrt2}{3}\times2\sqrt2{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{32}}{3}{/tex} sq units
solution_html:
We have, y2 = 8x and x = 2

{tex}= 4.\sqrt 2 .\left[ {\frac{{{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{\frac{3}{2}}} \right]_0^2 {/tex}

question_text: Sketch the region {tex}\left\{ {\left( {x,0} \right):y = \sqrt {4 - {x^2}} } \right\}{/tex} and x-axis. Find the area of the region using integration.
question_html: Sketch the region {tex}\left\{ {\left( {x,0} \right):y = \sqrt {4 - {x^2}} } \right\}{/tex}and x-axis. Find the area of the region using integration.
solution_text: Given region is {tex}\left\{ {\left( {x,0} \right):y = \sqrt {4 - {x^2}} } \right\}{/tex} and X-axis. We have, {tex}y = \sqrt {4 - {x^2}} \Rightarrow {y^2} = 4 - {x^2} \Rightarrow {x^2} + {y^2} = 4{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of shaded region, {tex}A = \int_{ - 2}^2 {\sqrt {4 - {x^2}} dx = \int_{ - 2}^2 {\sqrt {{2^2} - {x^2}} dx} } {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{x}{2}\sqrt {{2^2} - {x^2}} + \frac{{{2^2}}}{2}.{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{2}} \right]_{ - 2}^2{/tex} {tex}= \frac{2}{2}.0 + 2.\frac{\pi }{2} + \frac{2}{2}.0 - 2{\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( { - 1} \right) = 2.\frac{\pi }{2} + 2.\frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex} = 2\pi {/tex} sq units.
solution_html:
Given region is {tex}\left\{ {\left( {x,0} \right):y = \sqrt {4 - {x^2}} } \right\}{/tex}and X-axis.
We have, {tex}y = \sqrt {4 - {x^2}} \Rightarrow {y^2} = 4 - {x^2} \Rightarrow {x^2} + {y^2} = 4{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of shaded region, {tex}A = \int_{ - 2}^2 {\sqrt {4 - {x^2}} dx = \int_{ - 2}^2 {\sqrt {{2^2} - {x^2}} dx} } {/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {\frac{x}{2}\sqrt {{2^2} - {x^2}} + \frac{{{2^2}}}{2}.{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{2}} \right]_{ - 2}^2{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{2}{2}.0 + 2.\frac{\pi }{2} + \frac{2}{2}.0 - 2{\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( { - 1} \right) = 2.\frac{\pi }{2} + 2.\frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} 
{tex} = 2\pi {/tex} sq units.

question_html: Using integration, find the area of the region bounded by the line 2y = 5x + 7, x - axis and the lines x = 2 and x = 8.
solution_text: We have 2y = 5x + 7 {tex}\Rightarrow y = \frac{{5x}}{2} + \frac{7}{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of shaded region {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int_2^8 {\left( {5x + 7} \right)dx = \frac{1}{2}\left[ {5.\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 7x} \right]_2^8} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {5.32 + 7.8 - 10 - 14} \right] = \frac{1}{2}\left[ {160 + 56 - 24} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{192}}{2} = 96{/tex} sq units
solution_html:
We have 2y = 5x + 7
{tex}\Rightarrow y = \frac{{5x}}{2} + \frac{7}{2}{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of shaded region {tex}= \frac{1}{2}\int_2^8 {\left( {5x + 7} \right)dx = \frac{1}{2}\left[ {5.\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 7x} \right]_2^8} {/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2}\left[ {5.32 + 7.8 - 10 - 14} \right] = \frac{1}{2}\left[ {160 + 56 - 24} \right]{/tex}

question_text: Determine the area under the curve {tex}y = \sqrt {{a^2} - {x^2}} {/tex} included between the lines x = 0 and x = a.
question_html: Determine the area under the curve {tex}y = \sqrt {{a^2} - {x^2}} {/tex} included between the lines x = 0 and x = a.
solution_text: Given equation of the curve is {tex}y = \sqrt {{a^2} - {x^2}} {/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {y^2} = {a^2} - {x^2} \Rightarrow {y^2} + {x^2} = {a^2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area of shaded region, {tex}A = \int_0^a {\sqrt {{a^2} - {x^2}} dx} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{x}{2}\sqrt {{a^2} - {x^2}} + \frac{{{a^2}}}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{2}} \right]_0^a{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {0 + \frac{{{a^2}}}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( 1 \right) - 0 - \frac{{{a^2}}}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}0} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{a^2}}}{2}.\frac{\pi }{2} = \frac{{\pi {a^2}}}{4}{/tex} sq units.
solution_html:
Given equation of the curve is {tex}y = \sqrt {{a^2} - {x^2}} {/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow {y^2} = {a^2} - {x^2} \Rightarrow {y^2} + {x^2} = {a^2}{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area of shaded region, {tex}A = \int_0^a {\sqrt {{a^2} - {x^2}} dx} {/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {\frac{x}{2}\sqrt {{a^2} - {x^2}} + \frac{{{a^2}}}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{2}} \right]_0^a{/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {0 + \frac{{{a^2}}}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( 1 \right) - 0 - \frac{{{a^2}}}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}0} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{{{a^2}}}{2}.\frac{\pi }{2} = \frac{{\pi {a^2}}}{4}{/tex} sq units.

question_text: Find the area of the region bounded by {tex}y = \sqrt x {/tex} and y = x.
question_html: Find the area of the region bounded by {tex}y = \sqrt x {/tex} and y = x.
solution_text: Given equation of curves are {tex}y = \sqrt x {/tex} and y = x {tex} \Rightarrow x = \sqrt x \Rightarrow {x^2} = x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {x^2} - x = 0 \Rightarrow x\left( {x - 1} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x = 0,1{/tex} {tex}\therefore {/tex} The required area of the shaded region, {tex}A = \int_0^1 {\left( {\sqrt x } \right)dx - \int_0^1 {xdx} } {/tex} {tex} = \left[ {2.\frac{{{x^{3/2}}}}{3}} \right]_0^1 - \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_0^1{/tex} {tex}= \frac{2}{3}.1 - \frac{1}{2} = \frac{2}{3} - \frac{1}{2} = \frac{1}{6}{/tex} sq units.
solution_html:
Given equation of curves are {tex}y = \sqrt x {/tex} and y = x
{tex} \Rightarrow x = \sqrt x \Rightarrow {x^2} = x{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow {x^2} - x = 0 \Rightarrow x\left( {x - 1} \right) = 0{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow x = 0,1{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore {/tex} The required area of the shaded region, {tex}A = \int_0^1 {\left( {\sqrt x } \right)dx - \int_0^1 {xdx} } {/tex} 
{tex} = \left[ {2.\frac{{{x^{3/2}}}}{3}} \right]_0^1 - \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_0^1{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{2}{3}.1 - \frac{1}{2} = \frac{2}{3} - \frac{1}{2} = \frac{1}{6}{/tex}sq units.

question_text: Find the area enclosed by the curve y = -x 2 and the straight line x + y + 2 = 0.
question_html: Find the area enclosed by the curve y = -x2 and the straight line x + y + 2 = 0.
solution_text: We have, y = -x 2 and x + y + 2 = 0 {tex} \Rightarrow - x - 2 = - {x^2} \Rightarrow {x^2} - x - 2 = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {x^2} + x - 2x - 2 = 0 \Rightarrow x\left( {x + 1} \right) - 2\left( {x + 1} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \left( {x- 2} \right)\left( {x + 1} \right) = 0 \Rightarrow x = 2, - 1{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of shaded region, {tex}A = \left| {\int_{ - 1}^2 {\left( { - x - 2 + {x^2}} \right)dx} } \right| = \left| {\int_{ - 1}^2 {\left( {{x^2} - x - 2} \right)} dx} \right|{/tex} {tex}= \left| {\left[ {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - 2x} \right]} \right|_{ - 1}^2 {/tex} {tex}= \left| {\left[ {\frac{8}{3} - \frac{4}{2} - 4 + \frac{1}{3} + \frac{1}{2} - 2} \right]} \right|{/tex} {tex}= \left| {\frac{{16 - 12 - 24 + 2 + 3 - 12}}{6}} \right| = \left| { - \frac{{27}}{6}} \right| = \frac{9}{2}{/tex} sq units
solution_html:
We have, y = -x2 and x + y + 2 = 0

{tex} \Rightarrow - x - 2 = - {x^2} \Rightarrow {x^2} - x - 2 = 0{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow {x^2} + x - 2x - 2 = 0 \Rightarrow x\left( {x + 1} \right) - 2\left( {x + 1} \right) = 0{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow \left( {x- 2} \right)\left( {x + 1} \right) = 0 \Rightarrow x = 2, - 1{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of shaded region, {tex}A = \left| {\int_{ - 1}^2 {\left( { - x - 2 + {x^2}} \right)dx} } \right| = \left| {\int_{ - 1}^2 {\left( {{x^2} - x - 2} \right)} dx} \right|{/tex} 
{tex}= \left| {\left[ {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - 2x} \right]} \right|_{ - 1}^2 {/tex} {tex}= \left| {\left[ {\frac{8}{3} - \frac{4}{2} - 4 + \frac{1}{3} + \frac{1}{2} - 2} \right]} \right|{/tex} 
{tex}= \left| {\frac{{16 - 12 - 24 + 2 + 3 - 12}}{6}} \right| = \left| { - \frac{{27}}{6}} \right| = \frac{9}{2}{/tex}sq units

question_text: Find the area bounded by the curve {tex}y = \sqrt x,{/tex} x = 2y + 3 in the first quadrant and x-axis.
question_html: Find the area bounded by the curve {tex}y = \sqrt x,{/tex} x = 2y + 3 in the first quadrant and x-axis.
solution_text: Given equation of the curves are for {tex}y = \sqrt x ,{/tex} x = 2y + 3 in the first quadrant. On solving both the equation for y, we get {tex}y = \sqrt {2y + 3} {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {y^2} = 2y + 3{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {y^2} - 2y - 3 = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {y^2} - 3y + y - 3 = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y\left( {y - 3} \right) + 1\left( {y - 3} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left( {y + 1} \right)\left( {y - 3} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y = - 1,3{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area of shaded region, {tex}A = \int_0^3 {\left( {2y + 3 - {y^2}} \right)dy = \left[ {\frac{{2{y^2}}}{2} + 3y - \frac{{{y^3}}}{3}} \right]_0^3} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{{18}}{2} + 9 - 9 - 0} \right] = 9{/tex} sq units
solution_html:
Given equation of the curves are for {tex}y = \sqrt x ,{/tex} x = 2y + 3 in the first quadrant.
On solving both the equation for y, we get
{tex}y = \sqrt {2y + 3} {/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow {y^2} = 2y + 3{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow {y^2} - 2y - 3 = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {y^2} - 3y + y - 3 = 0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow y\left( {y - 3} \right) + 1\left( {y - 3} \right) = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left( {y + 1} \right)\left( {y - 3} \right) = 0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow y = - 1,3{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area of shaded region,
{tex}A = \int_0^3 {\left( {2y + 3 - {y^2}} \right)dy = \left[ {\frac{{2{y^2}}}{2} + 3y - \frac{{{y^3}}}{3}} \right]_0^3} {/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {\frac{{18}}{2} + 9 - 9 - 0} \right] = 9{/tex} sq units

question_text: Find the area of the region bounded by the curve y 2 = 2x and x 2 + y 2 = 4x.
question_html: Find the area of the region bounded by the curve y2 = 2x and x2 + y2 = 4x.
solution_text: We have, y 2 = 2x and x 2 + y 2 = 4x {tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} + 2x = 4x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {x^2} - 2x = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x\left( {x - 2} \right) = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x = 0,2{/tex} Also, {tex}{x^2} + {y^2} = 4x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} - 4x = - {y^2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} - 4x + 4 = - {y^2} + 4{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {\left( {x - 2} \right)^2} - {2^2} = - {y^2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area {tex} = 2\int_0^2 {\left[ {\sqrt {{2^2} - {{\left( {x - 2} \right)}^2}} - \sqrt 2 x} \right]} dx{/tex} {tex}= 2\left[ {\left[ {\frac{{x - 2}}{2}\sqrt {{2^2} - {{\left( {x - 2} \right)}^2}} + \frac{{{2^2}}}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}{{\left( {\frac{{x - 2}}{2}} \right)}^2}} \right] - \left[ {\sqrt 2 .\frac{{{x^{3/2}}}}{{3/2}}} \right]_0^2} \right]{/tex} {tex} = 2\left[ {\left( {0 + 0 - 1.0 + 2.\frac{\pi }{2}} \right) - \frac{{2\sqrt 2 }}{3}\left( {{2^{3/2}} - 0} \right)} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{4\pi }}{2} - \frac{{8.2}}{3} = 2\pi - \frac{{16}}{3} = 2\left( {\pi - \frac{8}{3}} \right){/tex} sq units
solution_html:
We have, y2 = 2x and x2 + y2 = 4x

{tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} + 2x = 4x{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow {x^2} - 2x = 0{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow x\left( {x - 2} \right) = 0{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow x = 0,2{/tex} 
Also, {tex}{x^2} + {y^2} = 4x{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} - 4x = - {y^2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} - 4x + 4 = - {y^2} + 4{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow {\left( {x - 2} \right)^2} - {2^2} = - {y^2}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area {tex} = 2\int_0^2 {\left[ {\sqrt {{2^2} - {{\left( {x - 2} \right)}^2}} - \sqrt 2 x} \right]} dx{/tex} 
{tex}= 2\left[ {\left[ {\frac{{x - 2}}{2}\sqrt {{2^2} - {{\left( {x - 2} \right)}^2}} + \frac{{{2^2}}}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}{{\left( {\frac{{x - 2}}{2}} \right)}^2}} \right] - \left[ {\sqrt 2 .\frac{{{x^{3/2}}}}{{3/2}}} \right]_0^2} \right]{/tex} 
{tex} = 2\left[ {\left( {0 + 0 - 1.0 + 2.\frac{\pi }{2}} \right) - \frac{{2\sqrt 2 }}{3}\left( {{2^{3/2}} - 0} \right)} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{{4\pi }}{2} - \frac{{8.2}}{3} = 2\pi - \frac{{16}}{3} = 2\left( {\pi - \frac{8}{3}} \right){/tex}sq units

question_text: Find the area bounded by the curve y = sin x between x = 0 and {tex}x = 2\pi {/tex}
question_html: Find the area bounded by the curve y = sin x between x = 0  and {tex}x = 2\pi {/tex}
solution_text: Equation of the curve is y = sin x …(i) {tex}\therefore y = \sin x \geqslant 0{/tex} for {tex}0 \leqslant x \leqslant \pi {/tex} i.e., graph is in first and second quadrant. And {tex}y = \sin x \leqslant 0{/tex} for {tex}\pi \leqslant x \leqslant 2\pi {/tex} i.e., graph is in third and fourth quadrant. If tangent is parallel to x - axis, then {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \cos x = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x = \frac{\pi }{2},\frac{{3\pi }}{2}{/tex} Table of values for curve y = sin x between x = 0 and {tex}x = 2\pi {/tex} x 0 {tex}\frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} {tex}\pi {/tex} {tex}\frac{{3\pi }}{2}{/tex} {tex}2\pi {/tex} y 0 1 0 - 1 0 Now Required shaded area = Area OAB + Area BCD {tex}= \left| {\int\limits_0^\pi {ydx} } \right| + \left| {\int\limits_\pi ^{2\pi } {ydx} } \right|{/tex} {tex}= \left| {\int\limits_0^\pi {\sin xdx} } \right| + \left| {\int\limits_\pi ^{2\pi } {\sin xdx} } \right|{/tex} {tex}= \left| { - \left( {\cos x} \right)_0^\pi } \right| + \left| { - \left( {\cos x} \right)_\pi ^{2\pi }} \right|{/tex} {tex}= \left| { - \left( {\cos \pi - \cos 0} \right)} \right| + \left| { - \left( {\cos 2\pi - \cos \pi } \right)} \right|{/tex} {tex}= \left| { - \left( { - 1 - 1} \right)} \right| + \left| { - \left( {1 + 1} \right)} \right|{/tex} = 2 + 2 = 4 sq. units
solution_html:
Equation of the curve is y = sin x …(i)
{tex}\therefore y = \sin x \geqslant 0{/tex} for {tex}0 \leqslant x \leqslant \pi {/tex} i.e., graph is in first and second quadrant.
And {tex}y = \sin x \leqslant 0{/tex} for {tex}\pi \leqslant x \leqslant 2\pi {/tex} i.e., graph is in third and fourth quadrant.

If tangent is parallel to x - axis, then {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 0{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \cos x = 0{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow x = \frac{\pi }{2},\frac{{3\pi }}{2}{/tex} 
Table of values for curve y = sin x between x = 0 and {tex}x = 2\pi {/tex}

x

0

{tex}\frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}

{tex}\pi {/tex}

{tex}\frac{{3\pi }}{2}{/tex}

{tex}2\pi {/tex}

y

0

1

0

- 1

0

Now Required shaded area = Area OAB + Area BCD
{tex}= \left| {\int\limits_0^\pi {ydx} } \right| + \left| {\int\limits_\pi ^{2\pi } {ydx} } \right|{/tex} 
{tex}= \left| {\int\limits_0^\pi {\sin xdx} } \right| + \left| {\int\limits_\pi ^{2\pi } {\sin xdx} } \right|{/tex} 
{tex}= \left| { - \left( {\cos x} \right)_0^\pi } \right| + \left| { - \left( {\cos x} \right)_\pi ^{2\pi }} \right|{/tex} 
{tex}= \left| { - \left( {\cos \pi - \cos 0} \right)} \right| + \left| { - \left( {\cos 2\pi - \cos \pi } \right)} \right|{/tex} 
{tex}= \left| { - \left( { - 1 - 1} \right)} \right| + \left| { - \left( {1 + 1} \right)} \right|{/tex} 
= 2 + 2 = 4 sq. units

question_text: Find the area of region bounded by the triangle whose vertices are (-1, 1), (0, 5) and (3, 2) using integration.
question_html: Find the area of region bounded by the triangle whose vertices are (-1, 1), (0, 5) and (3, 2) using integration.
solution_text: Let the vertices of a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} be A( - 1,1), B(0,5) and C(3, 2) {tex}\therefore{/tex} Equation of AB is {tex}y - 1 = \left( {\frac{{5 - 1}}{{0 + 1}}} \right)\left( {x + 1} \right){/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow y - 1 = 4x + 4{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow y = 4x + 5{/tex} ...(i) And equation of BC is {tex}y - 5 = \left( {\frac{{2 - 5}}{{3 - 0}}} \right)\left( {x - 0} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y - 5 = \frac{{ - 3}}{3}\left( x \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y = 5 - x{/tex} ...(ii) Similarly, the equation of AC is {tex}y - 1 = \left( {\frac{{2 - 1}}{{3 + 1}}} \right)\left( {x + 1} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow y - 1 = \frac{1}{4}\left( {x + 1} \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 4y = x + 5{/tex} ...(iii) {tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of shaded region {tex}= \int_{ - 1}^0 {\left( {{y_1} - {y_2}} \right)dx + \int_0^3 {\left( {{y_1} - {y_2}} \right)d} } x{/tex} {tex}= \int_{ - 1}^0 {\left[ {4x + 5 - \frac{{x + 5}}{4}} \right]dx + \int_0^3 {\left[ {5 - x - \frac{{x + 5}}{4}} \right]} }d x{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{{4{x^2}}}{2} + 5x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{8} - \frac{{5x}}{4}} \right]_{ - 1}^0 + \left[ {5x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - \frac{{{x^2}}}{8} - \frac{{5x}}{4}} \right]_0^3{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {0 - \left( {4.\frac{1}{2} + \left( { - 1} \right) + \frac{1}{8} + \frac{5}{4}} \right)} \right] + \left[ {\left( {15 - \frac{9}{2} - \frac{9}{8} - \frac{{15}}{4}} \right) - 0} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ { - 2 + 5 + \frac{1}{8} - \frac{5}{4} + 15 - \frac{9}{2} - \frac{9}{8} - \frac{{15}}{4}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= 18 + \left( {\frac{{1 - 10 - 36 - 9 - 30}}{8}} \right){/tex} {tex} = 18 + \left( { - \frac{{84}}{8}} \right) = 18 - \frac{{21}}{2} = \frac{{15}}{2}{/tex} sq units.
solution_html:
Let the vertices of a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} be A( - 1,1), B(0,5) and C(3, 2)

{tex}\therefore{/tex} Equation of AB is {tex}y - 1 = \left( {\frac{{5 - 1}}{{0 + 1}}} \right)\left( {x + 1} \right){/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow y - 1 = 4x + 4{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow y = 4x + 5{/tex} ...(i)
And equation of BC is {tex}y - 5 = \left( {\frac{{2 - 5}}{{3 - 0}}} \right)\left( {x - 0} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow y - 5 = \frac{{ - 3}}{3}\left( x \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow y = 5 - x{/tex} ...(ii)
Similarly, the equation of AC is {tex}y - 1 = \left( {\frac{{2 - 1}}{{3 + 1}}} \right)\left( {x + 1} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow y - 1 = \frac{1}{4}\left( {x + 1} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 4y = x + 5{/tex} ...(iii)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of shaded region {tex}= \int_{ - 1}^0 {\left( {{y_1} - {y_2}} \right)dx + \int_0^3 {\left( {{y_1} - {y_2}} \right)d} } x{/tex} 
{tex}= \int_{ - 1}^0 {\left[ {4x + 5 - \frac{{x + 5}}{4}} \right]dx + \int_0^3 {\left[ {5 - x - \frac{{x + 5}}{4}} \right]} }d x{/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {\frac{{4{x^2}}}{2} + 5x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{8} - \frac{{5x}}{4}} \right]_{ - 1}^0 + \left[ {5x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - \frac{{{x^2}}}{8} - \frac{{5x}}{4}} \right]_0^3{/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {0 - \left( {4.\frac{1}{2} + \left( { - 1} \right) + \frac{1}{8} + \frac{5}{4}} \right)} \right] + \left[ {\left( {15 - \frac{9}{2} - \frac{9}{8} - \frac{{15}}{4}} \right) - 0} \right]{/tex}  
{tex}= \left[ { - 2 + 5 + \frac{1}{8} - \frac{5}{4} + 15 - \frac{9}{2} - \frac{9}{8} - \frac{{15}}{4}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}= 18 + \left( {\frac{{1 - 10 - 36 - 9 - 30}}{8}} \right){/tex} 
{tex} = 18 + \left( { - \frac{{84}}{8}} \right) = 18 - \frac{{21}}{2} = \frac{{15}}{2}{/tex}sq units.

question_text: Find the area of the region {(x, y): y 2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 6ax and x 2 + y 2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 16a 2 }. Also find the area of the region sketched using method of integration.
question_html: Find the area of the region {(x, y): y2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 6ax and x2 + y2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 16a2}. Also find the area of the region sketched using method of integration.
solution_text: We have, y 2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 6ax, which represents the region interior to parabola y 2 = 6ax towards focus. And x 2 + y 2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 16a 2 , which represents the region interior to circle x 2 + y 2 = 16a 2 . Solving circle and parabola, we get x 2 + 6ax = 16a 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 + 6ax - 16a 2 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 2a) (x + 8a) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2a (as x = -8a is not possible) Putting x = 2a in a parabola, we get the graph of functions are as shown in the adjacent figure. From the figure, the area of the shaded region A = {tex}2\left[\int_{0}^{2 a} \sqrt{6 a x} d x+\int_{2 a}^{4a} \sqrt{(4 a)^{2}-x^{2}} d x\right]{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\sqrt{6 a}\left(\frac{2}{3} x^{3 / 2}\right)_{0}^{2 a}+\left(\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{(4 a)^{2}-x^{2}}+\frac{(4 a)^{2}}{2} \sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{4 a}\right)_{2 a}^{4 a}\right]{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\sqrt{6 a} \frac{2}{3}(2 a)^{3 / 2}+8 a^{2} \cdot \frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{2 a}{2} \sqrt{16 a^{2}-4 a^{2}}-8 a^{2} \cdot \frac{\pi}{6}\right]{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\sqrt{6 a} \frac{2}{3} \cdot 2 \sqrt{2} a^{3 / 2}+4 \pi a^{2}-a \cdot 2 \sqrt{3} a-\frac{4 a^{2}}{3} \pi\right]{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac{8}{3} \sqrt{3} a^{2}+4 \pi a^{2}-2 \sqrt{3} a^{2}-\frac{4 a^{2} \pi}{3}\right]{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac{2}{3} \sqrt{3} a^{2}+\frac{8 a^{2} \pi}{3}\right]=\frac{4}{3} a^{2}[\sqrt{3}+4 \pi]{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, y2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 6ax, which represents the region interior to parabola y2 = 6ax towards focus.
And x2 + y2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 16a2, which represents the region interior to circle x2 + y2 = 16a2. Solving circle and parabola, we get
x2 + 6ax = 16a2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 + 6ax - 16a2 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 2a) (x + 8a) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2a
(as x = -8a is not possible)
Putting x = 2a in a parabola, we get the graph of functions are as shown in the adjacent figure.

From the figure, the area of the shaded region
A = {tex}2\left[\int_{0}^{2 a} \sqrt{6 a x} d x+\int_{2 a}^{4a} \sqrt{(4 a)^{2}-x^{2}} d x\right]{/tex}
= {tex}2\left[\sqrt{6 a}\left(\frac{2}{3} x^{3 / 2}\right)_{0}^{2 a}+\left(\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{(4 a)^{2}-x^{2}}+\frac{(4 a)^{2}}{2} \sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{4 a}\right)_{2 a}^{4 a}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}2\left[\sqrt{6 a} \frac{2}{3}(2 a)^{3 / 2}+8 a^{2} \cdot \frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{2 a}{2} \sqrt{16 a^{2}-4 a^{2}}-8 a^{2} \cdot \frac{\pi}{6}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}2\left[\sqrt{6 a} \frac{2}{3} \cdot 2 \sqrt{2} a^{3 / 2}+4 \pi a^{2}-a \cdot 2 \sqrt{3} a-\frac{4 a^{2}}{3} \pi\right]{/tex}
= {tex}2\left[\frac{8}{3} \sqrt{3} a^{2}+4 \pi a^{2}-2 \sqrt{3} a^{2}-\frac{4 a^{2} \pi}{3}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}2\left[\frac{2}{3} \sqrt{3} a^{2}+\frac{8 a^{2} \pi}{3}\right]=\frac{4}{3} a^{2}[\sqrt{3}+4 \pi]{/tex}

question_text: Compute the area bounded by the lines x + 2y = 2, y – x = 1 and 2x + y = 7
question_html: Compute the area bounded by the lines x + 2y = 2, y – x = 1 and 2x + y = 7
solution_text: {tex}x + 2y = 2{/tex} ...(1) {tex}y - x = 1{/tex} ...(2) {tex}2x + y = 7{/tex} ...(3) Area {tex}= \int_0^2 {\left[ {\left( {1 + x} \right) + \left( {\frac{{2 - x}}{2}} \right)} \right]} dx + \int_2^4 {\left[ {\left( {7 - 2x} \right) - \left( {\frac{{2 - x}}{2}} \right)} \right]dx} {/tex} {tex}=\int_0^2\frac{3x}{2}dx+\int_2^4(6-\frac{3x}{2})dx{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{3x^2}{4}} \right] _{0}^{2} +\left[ {6x-\frac{3x^2}{4}} \right] _{2}^{4}{/tex} = 6 sq. unit
solution_html:
{tex}x + 2y = 2{/tex}...(1)
{tex}y - x = 1{/tex}...(2)
{tex}2x + y = 7{/tex} ...(3)
Area {tex}= \int_0^2 {\left[ {\left( {1 + x} \right) + \left( {\frac{{2 - x}}{2}} \right)} \right]} dx + \int_2^4 {\left[ {\left( {7 - 2x} \right) - \left( {\frac{{2 - x}}{2}} \right)} \right]dx} {/tex} 
{tex}=\int_0^2\frac{3x}{2}dx+\int_2^4(6-\frac{3x}{2})dx{/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {\frac{3x^2}{4}} \right] _{0}^{2} +\left[ {6x-\frac{3x^2}{4}} \right] _{2}^{4}{/tex} 
= 6 sq. unit

question_text: Find the area bounded by the lines y = 4x + 5, y = 5 - x and 4y = x + 5.
question_html: Find the area bounded by the lines y = 4x + 5, y = 5 - x and 4y = x + 5.
solution_text: Given equations of lines are y = 4x + 5 ...(i) y = 5 - x ...(ii) and 4y = x + 5 ...(iii) On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get 4x + 5 = 5 - x {tex} \Rightarrow x = 0{/tex} On solving Eqs. (i) and (iii) 4(4x + 5) = x + 5 {tex} \Rightarrow 16x + 20 = x + 5{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 15x = - 15{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x = - 1{/tex} On solving Eqs. (ii) and (iii), we get 4(5 - x) = x + 5 {tex}\Rightarrow 20 - 4x = x + 5{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x = 3{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area {tex}= \int_{ - 1}^0 {\left( {4x + 5} \right)dx + \int_0^3 {\left( {5 - x} \right)dx - \frac{1}{4}\int_{ - 1}^3 {\left( {x + 5} \right)dx} } } {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{{4{x^2}}}{2} + 5x} \right]_{ - 1}^0 + \left[ {5x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_0^3 - \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 5x} \right]_{ - 1}^3{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {0 - 2 + 5} \right] + \left[ {15 - \frac{9}{2} - 0} \right] - \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\frac{9}{2} + 15 - \frac{1}{2} + 5} \right]{/tex} {tex} = 3 + \frac{{21}}{2} - \frac{1}{4}.24{/tex} {tex}= - 3 + \frac{{21}}{2} = \frac{{15}}{2}{/tex} sq units
solution_html:
Given equations of lines are
y = 4x + 5 ...(i)
y = 5 - x ...(ii) and
4y = x + 5 ...(iii)
On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
4x + 5 = 5 - x
{tex} \Rightarrow x = 0{/tex} 
On solving Eqs. (i) and (iii)
4(4x + 5) = x + 5
{tex} \Rightarrow 16x + 20 = x + 5{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 15x = - 15{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow x = - 1{/tex} 
On solving Eqs. (ii) and (iii), we get
4(5 - x) = x + 5
{tex}\Rightarrow 20 - 4x = x + 5{/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow x = 3{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required area {tex}= \int_{ - 1}^0 {\left( {4x + 5} \right)dx + \int_0^3 {\left( {5 - x} \right)dx - \frac{1}{4}\int_{ - 1}^3 {\left( {x + 5} \right)dx} } } {/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {\frac{{4{x^2}}}{2} + 5x} \right]_{ - 1}^0 + \left[ {5x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_0^3 - \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 5x} \right]_{ - 1}^3{/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {0 - 2 + 5} \right] + \left[ {15 - \frac{9}{2} - 0} \right] - \frac{1}{4}\left[ {\frac{9}{2} + 15 - \frac{1}{2} + 5} \right]{/tex} 
{tex} = 3 + \frac{{21}}{2} - \frac{1}{4}.24{/tex} 
{tex}= - 3 + \frac{{21}}{2} = \frac{{15}}{2}{/tex}sq units

question_html: Draw a rough sketch of the given curve y = 1 + |x + 1|, x = - 3, x = 3, y = 0 and find the area of the region bounded by them, using integration.
solution_text: We have, y = 1 + |x + 1|, x = - 3, x = 3, y = 0 {tex}\therefore y = \left\{ \begin{gathered} - x,if\,\,x < - 1 \hfill \\ x + 2,\,\,if\,x \geqslant - 1 \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right.{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of shaded region, {tex}A = \int_{ - 3}^{ - 1} { - xdx + \int_{ - 1}^3 {\left( {x + 2} \right)dx} } {/tex} {tex}= - \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_{ - 3}^{ - 1} + \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 2x} \right]_{ - 1}^3{/tex} {tex}= - \left[ {\frac{1}{2} - \frac{9}{2}} \right] + \left[ {\frac{9}{2} + 6 - \frac{1}{2} + 2} \right]{/tex} {tex}= - \left[ { - 4} \right] + \left[ {8 + 4} \right]{/tex} = 12 + 4 = 16 sq units
solution_html:
We have, y = 1 + |x + 1|, x = - 3, x = 3, y = 0

{tex}\therefore y = \left\{ \begin{gathered} - x,if\,\,x < - 1 \hfill \\ x + 2,\,\,if\,x \geqslant - 1 \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right.{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of shaded region, {tex}A = \int_{ - 3}^{ - 1} { - xdx + \int_{ - 1}^3 {\left( {x + 2} \right)dx} } {/tex} 
{tex}= - \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_{ - 3}^{ - 1} + \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} + 2x} \right]_{ - 1}^3{/tex} 
{tex}= - \left[ {\frac{1}{2} - \frac{9}{2}} \right] + \left[ {\frac{9}{2} + 6 - \frac{1}{2} + 2} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}= - \left[ { - 4} \right] + \left[ {8 + 4} \right]{/tex} 
= 12 + 4 = 16 sq units

question_text: Find the area of the region bounded by the parabola y 2 = 2x and the straight line x - y = 4.
question_html: Find the area of the region bounded by the parabola y2 = 2x and the straight line x - y = 4.
solution_text: The intersecting points of the given curves are obtained by solving the equations x - y = 4 and y 2 = 2x for x and y. We have y 2 = 8 + 2y, i.e (y - 4) (y + 2) = 0 which gives y = 4, -2 and x = 8, 2. Thus, the points of intersection are (8, 4), (2, -2). Hence Area {tex}= \int\limits_{ - 2}^4 {\left( {4 + y - \frac{1}{2}{y^2}} \right)dy} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {4y + \frac{{{y^2}}}{2} - \frac{1}{6}{y^3}} \right]_{ - 2}^4{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {4 (4)+ \frac{{{4^2}}}{2} - \frac{1}{6}{4^3}} \right]{/tex} {tex}- \left[ {4(-2) + \frac{{{(-2)^2}}}{2} - \frac{1}{6}{(-2)^3}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {16 + 8 - \frac{64}{6}{}} \right]-\left[ {-8 + 2 +\frac{8}{6}{}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {16 + 8+ 8-2- \frac{64}{6}-\frac{8}{6}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {30- \frac{72}{6}{}} \right]{/tex} = [30 - 12] = 18 sq units. Which is the required solution.
solution_html:
The intersecting points of the given curves are obtained by solving the equations x - y = 4 and y2 = 2x for x and y.
We have y2 = 8 + 2y, i.e (y - 4) (y + 2) = 0 which gives y = 4, -2 and x = 8, 2.
Thus, the points of intersection are (8, 4), (2, -2). Hence
Area {tex}= \int\limits_{ - 2}^4 {\left( {4 + y - \frac{1}{2}{y^2}} \right)dy} {/tex}
{tex}= \left[ {4y + \frac{{{y^2}}}{2} - \frac{1}{6}{y^3}} \right]_{ - 2}^4{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ {4 (4)+ \frac{{{4^2}}}{2} - \frac{1}{6}{4^3}} \right]{/tex}{tex}- \left[ {4(-2) + \frac{{{(-2)^2}}}{2} - \frac{1}{6}{(-2)^3}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ {16 + 8 - \frac{64}{6}{}} \right]-\left[ {-8 + 2 +\frac{8}{6}{}} \right]{/tex}

= [30 - 12] = 18 sq units.

Which is the required solution.

solution_text: Eliminating t as follows: x = 3 cos t, y = 2 sin t {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{x}{3} = \cos t,\frac{y}{2} = \sin t{/tex} we obtain {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{9} + \frac{{{y^2}}}{4} = 1,{/tex} which is the equation of an ellipse. We get the required area {tex}= 4\int\limits_3^3 {\frac{2}{3}\sqrt {9 - {x^2}} dx} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{8}{3}\left[ {\frac{x}{2}\sqrt {9 - {x^2}} + \frac{9}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{3}} \right]_0^3 {/tex} {tex}= \frac{8}{3}\left[ {(0+\frac{9}{2}×\frac{ {tex}\pi{/tex} }{2})-(0+0)} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{8}{3}\left[ {\frac{9 {tex}\pi{/tex} }{4}} \right]=6 {tex}\pi{/tex} {/tex} sq. units
solution_html:
Eliminating t as follows:
x = 3 cos t, y = 2 sin t {tex} \Rightarrow \frac{x}{3} = \cos t,\frac{y}{2} = \sin t{/tex}we obtain {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{9} + \frac{{{y^2}}}{4} = 1,{/tex}which is the equation of an ellipse.
We get the required area {tex}= 4\int\limits_3^3 {\frac{2}{3}\sqrt {9 - {x^2}} dx} {/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{8}{3}\left[ {\frac{x}{2}\sqrt {9 - {x^2}} + \frac{9}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{3}} \right]_0^3 {/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{8}{3}\left[ {(0+\frac{9}{2}×\frac{{tex}\pi{/tex}}{2})-(0+0)} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{8}{3}\left[ {\frac{9{tex}\pi{/tex}}{4}} \right]=6{tex}\pi{/tex}{/tex}sq. units

question_text: Find the area of the region included between the parabola {tex}y=\frac{3}{4}x^2{/tex} and the line 3x - 2y + 12 = 0
question_html: Find the area of the region included between the parabola {tex}y=\frac{3}{4}x^2{/tex} and the line 3x - 2y + 12 = 0
solution_text: The equations of given curves are {tex}y=\frac{3}{4}x^2{/tex} ............(1)} and {tex}3x-2y+12=0{/tex} ...........(2) From (2), {tex} 2y=3x+12{/tex} {tex}\therefore\ y=\frac{3x+12}{2}{/tex} putting this value of y in (1), we get {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{3x+12}{2}=\frac{3}{4}x^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 6x+24=3x^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x^2-2x-8=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow (x+2)(x-4)=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=-2,4{/tex} {tex}\therefore\ {/tex} {tex}y=3,12{/tex} Thus, curves (1) and (2) intersect in points A(4,12) and B(-2,3). From A, draw AM {tex}\perp{/tex} x-axis and from B, draw BN {tex}\perp{/tex} x-axis. Required area= the area of the region included between the parabola {tex}y=\frac{3}{4}x^2{/tex} and the line {tex} 3x-2y+12=0{/tex} = area of trapezium BNMA - (area BNO + area OMA) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}(3+12)×6-\int_{-2}^4\frac{3}{4}x^2\ dx{/tex} [using (1)] = {tex}45-\frac{3}{4}\int_{-2}^4x^2\ dx{/tex} = {tex}45-\frac{3}{4}\left[{\frac{x^3}{3}}\right]_{-2}^4{/tex} = {tex}45-\frac{1}{4}[x^3]_{-2}^4{/tex} = {tex}45-\frac{1}{4}[64+8]{/tex} = 27 sq.units.
solution_html:
The equations of given curves are
{tex}y=\frac{3}{4}x^2{/tex}............(1)}
and {tex}3x-2y+12=0{/tex} ...........(2)
From (2), {tex} 2y=3x+12{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore\ y=\frac{3x+12}{2}{/tex} 
putting this value of y in (1), we get
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{3x+12}{2}=\frac{3}{4}x^2{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 6x+24=3x^2{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow x^2-2x-8=0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow (x+2)(x-4)=0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow x=-2,4{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore\ {/tex}{tex}y=3,12{/tex} 
Thus, curves (1) and (2) intersect in points  A(4,12) and B(-2,3).

From A, draw AM{tex}\perp{/tex} x-axis and from B, draw BN{tex}\perp{/tex} x-axis.
Required area=  the area of the region included between the parabola {tex}y=\frac{3}{4}x^2{/tex} and the line {tex} 3x-2y+12=0{/tex} 
= area of trapezium BNMA - (area BNO + area OMA)
={tex}\frac{1}{2}(3+12)×6-\int_{-2}^4\frac{3}{4}x^2\ dx{/tex} [using (1)]
={tex}45-\frac{3}{4}\int_{-2}^4x^2\ dx{/tex} 
={tex}45-\frac{3}{4}\left[{\frac{x^3}{3}}\right]_{-2}^4{/tex} 
={tex}45-\frac{1}{4}[x^3]_{-2}^4{/tex} 
={tex}45-\frac{1}{4}[64+8]{/tex} 
= 27 sq.units.

question_html: Find the area of the region bounded by the curves x = at2 and y = 2 at between the ordinate corresponding to t = 1 and t = 2.
solution_text: Given that x = at 2 ...(i), y = 2at ..(ii) {tex} \Rightarrow t = \frac{y}{{2a}}{/tex} putting the value of t in (i), we get y 2 = 4ax Putting t = 1 and t = 2 in (i), we get x = a, and x = 4a Required area = 2 area of ABCD {tex}= 2\int\limits_a^{4a} {ydx = 2 \times 2\int\limits_a^{4a} {\sqrt {ax} dx} } {/tex} {tex}= 8\sqrt 2 \left| {\frac{{{{\left( x \right)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{3}} \right|_a^{4a} = \frac{{56}}{3}{a^2}{/tex} sq units

question_text: Find the area of the region above the x-axis, included between the parabola y 2 = ax and the circle x 2 + y 2 = 2ax.
question_html: Find the area of the region above the x-axis, included between the parabola y2 = ax and the circle x2 + y2 = 2ax.
solution_text: Solving the given equations of curves, we have x 2 + ax = 2ax or x = 0, x = a, which give y = 0. y = {tex}\pm{/tex} a area ODAB = {tex}\int_{0}^{a}(\sqrt{2 a x-x^{2}}-\sqrt{a x}) d x{/tex} Let x = 2a sin 2 {tex}\theta{/tex} . Then dx = 4a sin {tex}\theta{/tex} cos {tex}\theta{/tex} d {tex}\theta{/tex} and x = 0, {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = 0, x = a {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} Again, {tex}\int_{0}^{a} \sqrt{2 a x-x^{2}} d x{/tex} = {tex}a^{2} \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}(1-\cos 4 \theta) d \theta=a^{2}\left(\theta-\frac{\sin 4 \theta}{4}\right)_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}=\frac{\pi}{4} a^{2}{/tex} Furthermore, {tex}\int_{0}^{a} \sqrt{a x} d x{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{a} \frac{2}{3}\left(x^{\frac{3}{2}}\right)_{0}^{a}=\frac{2}{3} a^{2}{/tex} Thus the required area = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} a^{2}-\frac{2}{3} a^{2}{/tex} = {tex}a^{2}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{2}{3}\right){/tex} sq. units.
solution_html:
Solving the given equations of curves, we have
x2 + ax = 2ax
or x = 0, x = a, which give
y = 0. y = {tex}\pm{/tex} a
 
area ODAB = {tex}\int_{0}^{a}(\sqrt{2 a x-x^{2}}-\sqrt{a x}) d x{/tex} 
Let x = 2a sin2 {tex}\theta{/tex}. Then dx = 4a sin {tex}\theta{/tex} cos {tex}\theta{/tex} d{tex}\theta{/tex} and 
x = 0, {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = 0, x = a {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex} 
Again, {tex}\int_{0}^{a} \sqrt{2 a x-x^{2}} d x{/tex} 
= {tex}a^{2} \int_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}(1-\cos 4 \theta) d \theta=a^{2}\left(\theta-\frac{\sin 4 \theta}{4}\right)_{0}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}=\frac{\pi}{4} a^{2}{/tex} 
Furthermore, 
{tex}\int_{0}^{a} \sqrt{a x} d x{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{a} \frac{2}{3}\left(x^{\frac{3}{2}}\right)_{0}^{a}=\frac{2}{3} a^{2}{/tex} 
Thus the required area = {tex}\frac{\pi}{4} a^{2}-\frac{2}{3} a^{2}{/tex} = {tex}a^{2}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{2}{3}\right){/tex} sq. units.

question_html: Find the area of a minor segment of the circle x2 + y2 = a2 cut off by the line {tex}x = \frac{a}{2}.{/tex}
solution_text: Solving the equation x 2 + y 2 = a 2 and {tex}x = \frac{a}{2}{/tex} , we obtain their points of intersection which are {tex}\left( {\frac{a}{2},\sqrt 3 \frac{a}{2}} \right){/tex} and {tex}\left( {\frac{a}{2} - \frac{{\sqrt 3 a}}{2}} \right){/tex} . Hence, we get Required Area {tex}= 2\left[ {\frac{x}{2}\sqrt {{a^2} - {x^2}} + \frac{{{a^2}}}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{2}} \right]_{\frac{a}{2}}^a{/tex} {tex}= 2\left[ {\frac{{{a^2}}}{2}.\frac{\pi }{2} - \frac{a}{4}.a\frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2} - \frac{{{a^2}}}{2}.\frac{\pi }{6}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{{a^2}}}{{12}}\left( {6\pi - 3\sqrt 3 - 2\pi } \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{{{a^2}}}{{12}}\left( {4\pi - 3\sqrt 3 } \right){/tex} sq units

Hence,  we get
Required Area
{tex}= 2\left[ {\frac{x}{2}\sqrt {{a^2} - {x^2}} + \frac{{{a^2}}}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{2}} \right]_{\frac{a}{2}}^a{/tex} 
{tex}= 2\left[ {\frac{{{a^2}}}{2}.\frac{\pi }{2} - \frac{a}{4}.a\frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2} - \frac{{{a^2}}}{2}.\frac{\pi }{6}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex} = \frac{{{a^2}}}{{12}}\left( {6\pi - 3\sqrt 3 - 2\pi } \right){/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{{{a^2}}}{{12}}\left( {4\pi - 3\sqrt 3 } \right){/tex} sq units
